summary: Frontend-only E2E testing of Hospital Reception Management System (mock-data React app). All 10 critical flows verified via Playwright: redirect to /reception, sidebar nav + coming-soon, dashboard KPIs + quick actions, register w/ duplicate detection (+91 98201 33421 -> Anjali Krishnan MRN-100231), new-patient submit + navigate to /reception/patients (new patient appears at top), patient search + detail panel, appointments filters/doctor filter/prev-next-today/new-booking link, new appointment patient search + doctor + time slots rendered, check-in issues token (last-token-card appears with #102) and removes row from arrivals, queue call-next promotes waiting -> in-progress (complete button appears), transfer modal opens, token-display kiosk has no sidebar with massive token numbers and back/fullscreen buttons. All data-testid attributes verified.

issues: React hydration console warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' coming from Emergent dev wrapper injecting <span style=display:contents> inside <option> children. Likely a dev-tooling artifact (Made with Emergent) and not an app code bug, but it floods the console with errors. Worth confirming with E1 whether the wrapper config can be excluded for option children.

action_items: No functional fixes required - all core flows pass.

Optional: investigate the <span> inside <option> hydration warning (appears to originate from the Emergent visual-edit wrapper, not app source). If reproducible from app code, switch the <option> body to a plain string so the wrapper does not wrap it.
critical_code_review_comments: store.js: mrnCounter/aptCounter are module-level let variables -- across hot reload or multiple StoreProvider mounts they would not reset. Fine for current mock use but worth flagging.
checkInAppointment uses Math.max(...used) which crashes if used has >100k entries (not relevant for mock).
findDuplicate dereferences p.phone directly; if a future patient lacks a phone the .replace will throw. Add a null guard.
AppLayout clock interval runs at 15s -- minor: time tile may lag up to 15s, acceptable.
All interactive elements correctly expose kebab-case data-testid attributes.
test_credentials: Not required - app has no auth.
seed_data_creation: Created one in-session test patient 'TEST Auto Patient' (MRN-100239, phone +91 90000 00001) via Register form. Resets on page refresh because of React Context-only state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is frontend-only (React + React Context). No backend, no persistence across refresh. All data-testid selectors are kebab-case as documented in review_request. Token numbering scheme: per-doctor (DOC-001 -> 1xx, DOC-002 -> 2xx). Filter pill ids in Appointments use 'appt-filter-<kebab-status>' (e.g. appt-filter-checked-in, appt-filter-in-consult, appt-filter-no-show). Queue action ids are queue-callnext-<docId>, queue-complete-<docId>, queue-skip-<docId>, queue-recall-<aptId>, queue-transfer-<aptId>, queue-noshow-<aptId>, transfer-to-<docId>. Check-in row/btn ids: checkin-row-<aptId>, checkin-btn-<aptId>. Conflict warning selector 'newappt-conflict' exists in source but wasn't triggered in this run (didn't pick an exact occupied slot post patient+doctor selection); a deeper test could explicitly pick 09:00 for DOC-001 to verify.
rca_of_the_issue: No bugs found. Hydration warning RCA: the Emergent dev wrapper instruments JSX expressions by wrapping them in a <span style=display:contents>; when applied inside <option> children (NewAppointment.jsx doctor select), it produces invalid HTML (<span> inside <option>). Not an app source bug -- the source uses plain {d.name}{d.specialty} text. Mitigation if needed: rebuild option label as a single concatenated string before render, e.g. const label = `${d.name} - ${d.specialty}`; <option>{label}</option>.
